name: Braised Chinese Cabbage
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Wash and soak Chinese mushrooms and dried shrimp in warm water for 1 hour until soft. Mince the shrimp. Remove the hard stem from the mushrooms. Wash and thinly slice mustard greens. Separate cabbage leaves; blanch in salted boiling water for 30 seconds; drain; slice into 2-inch sections. In a bowl, combine stock, salt, sherry, sugar, and soy sauce; reserve. Render chicken fat in a small saucepan at medium heat; discard the pieces of fat; keep the oil hot.
Braising: Heat peanut oil in a wok until hot but not smoking. Stir-fry mushrooms, shrimp, and mustard greens for 15 seconds. Add cabbage; stir-fry for 1 minute. Add the stock mixture; bring to boil. Reduce heat, cover the wok, and simmer for 10 minutes, reducing liquid by half; add more stock if needed. Turn up the heat; add enough cornstarch paste to make a light sauce. Swirl in hot chicken oil. Serve.
Note: Cabbage leaves should become transparent, but avoid overcooking; they should be soft but not wilted.
